timeline.label: Q1 FY2026: revenue $1.04B (+6.2% YoY); diluted EPS $1.57 (+24%); OCF $151M vs $53M prior year — teen-case volume recovery and margin improvement
FY2025 results: revenue $3.86B; net income $445M; OCF $786M; repurchased 1.2M shares at avg $140.22
April 2025 share repurchase program authorized; continued aggressive buybacks alongside January 2023 program
Restructuring plans (2023–2025) reduced headcount and consolidated manufacturing; Juarez Mexico facility central to cost base
Antitrust class actions and Straumann litigation continue; Angelalign IP actions in China remain active risk items
Direct-to-consumer clear-aligner competition intensified post-SmileDirectClub era; ASP pressure on teen and adult cases
iTero intraoral scanner ecosystem expanded; Systems and Services segment grew as digital orthodontic workflow pillar
Invisalign clear aligner FDA clearance and global orthodontic franchise build; listed on NASDAQ as ALGN

industry.structure: Oligopoly in doctor-directed clear aligners: Align Technology (Invisalign) holds dominant share in orthodontist channel; Straumann, Envista, and regional players compete. High switching costs from scanner ecosystem and treatment planning software.
industry.share_shift: Align retains leadership in premium orthodontist segment; DTC and lower-price competitors pressure ASP in adult cases; international growth (China, EMEA) remains key volume driver.
industry.trend: Digital orthodontic workflow (iTero scans, 3D treatment planning) deepens doctor lock-in; teen-case adoption is cyclical; litigation and trade/regional risk (Israel, Russia, China) add uncertainty.
